meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Freelancers: Beat Slow Development Speed
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what your users want. Learn how to implement robust analytics fast using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development workflow.
content.answer_block: Slow development stems from building features users don't actually need. By integrating automated event-tracking analytics into your stack, you gain immediate, data-backed insights. Use Antigravity to scaffold your telemetry layer and Claude Code to deploy it across your codebase in minutes, ensuring every sprint delivers maximum client value.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: three weeks into a build, sweating over a feature that looked great in a wireframe but feels clunky in production. Your velocity is tanking because you’re flying blind, guessing which buttons get clicked and which user flows end in frustration. The silence from your client is louder than any bug report.

It’s time to stop building in the dark. Professional-grade analytics aren't just for 'Big Tech'; they are your competitive advantage as a freelancer. By embedding telemetry into your workflow today, you transform raw usage data into a roadmap for rapid, high-impact development that proves your worth to every client.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Event Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three 'North Star' actions that define user success. This prevents data bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and propose a lean, event-driven telemetry schema focusing on user onboarding, feature engagement, and conversion milestones.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a JSON schema for our analytics event tracking based on the current user flow'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized wrapper to handle event dispatching, ensuring you aren't leaking tracking logic throughout your UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust, type-safe middleware module for handling analytics event logging. Include batching capabilities to minimize network impact.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new utility file src/lib/analytics.ts that exports a trackEvent function'

steps.title: Inject Telemetry via Claude Code
steps.description: Use AI to automate the tedious task of attaching event listeners to your UI components without breaking existing functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit my main navigation and action components. Inject trackEvent calls for all primary user interactions identified in Step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scan all files in /components and add trackEvent calls to onClick handlers for buttons'

steps.title: Configure Automated Error Tracking
steps.description: Analytics aren't just for success; they are for catching silent failures before your client does.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an error boundary component that automatically triggers an analytics event with the stack trace whenever an unhandled exception occurs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Wrap the main app in an ErrorBoundary that logs to our telemetry service'

steps.title: Automate Weekly Insights Reporting
steps.description: Ship a summary script that parses your logs into a human-readable format for your client updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a node script that queries my analytics logs and generates a markdown summary of the top 5 most used features this week.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a script scripts/report.js that outputs a weekly usage summary'

pro_tips: Always track 'time-to-first-click' to identify friction in your onboarding flow.
Use feature flags in your analytics to toggle tracking on/off without redeploying code.
Implement 'breadcrumb' logging for critical errors to reconstruct the user's exact path before a crash.
Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information) to maintain GDPR compliance without extra legal overhead.
Trigger events on 'idle' states to catch users who are confused or stuck on specific pages.
content.next_steps: Once your telemetry is live, set up a dashboard in your preferred tool (like PostHog or Mixpanel) and review the data for 48 hours to identify the 'low-hanging fruit' features that need optimization.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start shipping with precision. Our community of high-velocity builders is sharing the exact templates and prompts you need to automate your stack and double your output. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
